idea.hook: Want to visit an alien world? How fast could we get there?
facts: Proxima Centauri b, our closest exoplanet, is 4.2 light-years away.
Traveling at the speed of light, that journey would take 4.2 years.
Our fastest spacecraft (Parker Solar Probe) reaches up to 195 km/s. At *that* speed, it would take over 6,500 years to reach!
idea.payoff: Even our closest alien neighbor is an impossible journey with current technology. Interstellar travel remains a distant dream!
idea.title: Closest Alien World: HOW LONG?! 🤯🚀

scenes.text: WANT TO VISIT AN ALIEN WORLD?
scenes.visual: A vibrant, abstract alien exoplanet (e.g., gas giant or rocky planet with rings) against a swirling nebula backdrop.

scenes.text: Our closest: Proxima Centauri b. 4.2 Light-Years Away.
scenes.visual: A simplified star map showing our Sun and Proxima Centauri with a dashed line connecting them, highlighting the distance.

scenes.text: Travel at LIGHT SPEED? That's 4.2 years.
scenes.visual: A beam of light traveling quickly across a starfield, illustrating the concept of light speed travel.

scenes.text: Our fastest ship (195 km/s) would take 6,500+ YEARS!
scenes.visual: An animated stylized icon of the Parker Solar Probe flying from a small Earth icon towards a distant star, with a very long, winding path.
The Earth appearing small and lonely, slowly receding into a vast, dark, star-filled space, emphasizing the immense distances.
